facts_disclosed: Switched to ABF Freight, 74 cents/mile, northeast routes, better benefits

turns.content: man i am wired right now its like 1am and im sitting at a pilot in virginia and i just cannot sleep because things are finally going my way for once you know
Hey! 1am at a truck stop buzzing with energy — sounds like something good happened. What's going on?

okay so you know how i been driving out of atlanta mostly southeast routes right well i just made a move that changes everything i put in my two weeks at southeastern and started with abf freight last monday
Oh wow, that's a big switch! ABF is a solid company. What made you pull the trigger on leaving?

bro the routes thats what did it abf runs me through the northeast like every other week which means i can swing through newark and see my daughter naomi and thats worth more than anything to me you know i was only seeing her maybe once a month before if i was lucky sometimes six weeks would go by and that was killing me inside

That's huge — going from once every 6 weeks to potentially every other week is a game changer for you and Naomi. Being able to build that consistency matters so much at her age. How's the pay compare to what you were making?

thats the beautiful part its not even a sacrifice pay wise they got me at 74 cents a mile which is up from 68 so thats like a real raise on top of the better routes and i was listening to this podcast called freightwaves the other day and they were talking about how the market is tightening up for drivers right now so its actually a good time to negotiate you know

74 cents a mile is a nice bump — that's almost a 9% raise just on the per-mile rate alone. Plus better routes that let you see your daughter more often. Sounds like you timed it perfectly with the market conditions too. How are the benefits compared to your old gig?

way better man thats actually the other thing that pushed me over my mom called me last week and she was like jerome you need real insurance with your cholesterol numbers the way they are and she is right you know southeastern had the bare minimum plan but abf has actual good health coverage dental vision the whole thing and i been meaning to get more serious about the cholesterol situation anyway so having insurance that actually covers the doctor visits and medication properly thats a big deal

Your mom sounds like a wise woman — she's not wrong about that. Good health coverage can make a real difference, especially with ongoing medication and monitoring. Being able to see a doctor regularly without worrying about the cost makes it way easier to actually stay on top of it. Did you have to go through any kind of transition period or did they put you right to work?

they had me do orientation in fort smith arkansas for a week which was boring as all get out but pa gen pwoblem you know i sat through it and smiled and now im rolling the routes are already better i just came down from connecticut through jersey and im heading back south through virginia right now and next run they already told me its going up through pennsylvania into new york so im going to try to time it to stop in newark for a night

That's a beautiful setup — Connecticut, Jersey, PA, New York. Those northeast corridors are going to keep putting you right in Naomi's orbit. Does she know you switched companies specifically to be closer to her?

nah i dont want to put that on her like that shes 9 you know i dont want her to feel like pressure or responsibility i just told her daddy is going to be coming through more often now and she was so happy she started listing all the things she wants to show me at her school and this park near her moms place bondye bon that little girl is everything to me

That's really thoughtful — letting her just enjoy the extra time without feeling the weight of it. And the fact that she immediately had a whole list ready tells you how much she's been wanting this too. She's probably been saving up things to show you.

exactly exactly thats what i think too like she had that list ready because shes been thinking about it and now i can actually be there for it you know some of the guys at southeastern were like man why would you leave you got seniority here and i was like bro seniority dont mean nothing if i cant see my kid growing up you know some things are more important than a comfortable routine

100%. Seniority is nice on paper, but you can't get those years with Naomi back. She's 9 now — these are the years she'll remember. Better pay, better benefits, better routes to see your daughter. Sounds like you made the right call on every level. I hope that first Newark stop goes perfectly.

man i hope so too im already planning it in my head like im going to bring her those little haitian patties from that bakery in east orange she loves those things okay i should probably try to sleep i got 400 miles tomorrow but i just had to tell somebody because im feeling good for the first time in a while sa k pase you know life is happening

Life IS happening, and it sounds like it's moving in a really good direction. Haitian patties from East Orange — she's going to love that. Get some rest, knock out those 400 miles tomorrow, and enjoy this chapter. You earned it.
